title: Bloodsniffer

'''Bloodsniffers''' were vicious predators that were among the many dangerous beasts that could be found in the desolate [[Kamar Badlands|Badlands]] of [[Kamar/Legends|Kamar]]. Possessing a metabolism that consumed [[potassium]] at an incredible rate, bloodsniffers ingested their full body weight in fresh [[Blood/Legends|blood]] every two [[Standard day/Legends|days]], piercing the veins and arteries of their victims with their long, sharp tongues. Bloodsniffers lived in small packs, and the territorial creatures were known to attack each other over the blood they needed to survive.

==Biology and appearance==
A short, muscular predator native to the [[Desert/Legends|deserts]] of the [[Wild Space/Legends|Wild Space]] [[Planet/Legends|planet]] of [[Kamar/Legends|Kamar]], bloodsniffers were so named because of their<ref name="hsc" /> keen senses<ref name="UA" /> and heavy consumption of their victims' [[Blood/Legends|blood]]: they consumed their weight in fresh blood every two [[Standard day/Legends|days]], owing to how quickly their metabolism consumed [[potassium]]. Bloodsniffers ingested blood to such an extent that they did not eat or drink [[Water/Legends|water]], as they received all the nutrients they needed from blood alone.<ref name="hsc" /> Although they appeared [[Lizard/Legends|lizard]]-like, bloodsniffers were actually mammals, and were distinguished by a long, sharpened tongue that facilitated their feeding. They moved about on four legs that ended in sharp claws, and had pure [[Color/Legends|white]] [[Eye/Legends|eyes]] that sat just below a set of small horns.<ref name="UA" />

==Behavior==
Extremely territorial, bloodsniffers tended to live in small packs, ranging from two to eight adults, plus pups. These packs were known to fight each other to the [[Death/Legends|death]] over<ref name="UA" /> Commonly found within the desolate [[Kamar Badlands]],<ref name="hsr">''[[Han Solo's Revenge]]''</ref> bloodsniffers were known to favor [[Mountain/Legends|mountainous]] areas as their preferred hunting ground, although they could thrive wherever they found enough prey to sustain their need for fresh blood. The bloodsniffer typically attacked its prey by tackling or clawing its victim, then using its pointed tongue to pierce a major vein or artery and drinking its prey dry.<ref name="hsc" /> This behavior proved to be a boon for scavengers, who often followed bloodsniffer packs, knowing that the predators would leave the flesh of their victims behind once they had their fill of blood.<ref name="UA" />

==History==
Although bloodsniffers were native to Kamar, their reputation spread as far as the [[Outer Rim Territories/Legends|Outer Rim's]] [[Quence sector/Legends|Quence sector]], where they were the namesake of a dangerous [[Bloodsniffers|swoop gang]] that plagued local villages during the [[Imperial Era/Legends|Imperial Period]].<ref name="gg9">''[[Galaxy Guide 9: Fragments from the Rim]]''</ref>

==Behind the scenes==
Bloodsniffers were first mentioned in ''[[Han Solo's Revenge]]'', a novel written by [[Brian Daley]] and released in [[1979]].<ref name="hsr" /> They were first pictured by [[Mike Vilardi]] in [[1993]]'s ''[[Han Solo and the Corporate Sector Sourcebook]]'',<ref name="hsc" /> and later by [[Matt Hatton]] in [[2004]]'s ''[[Ultimate Adversaries]]''.<ref name="UA" />
